1‚Üí# Frontend Style Guide ‚Äî Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíWelcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4‚ÜíThis version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5‚Üí
     6‚Üí> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ‚òï
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí---
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí- [Introduction](#introduction)
    13‚Üí- [Components & Screens ‚Äî Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    14‚Üí- [Atomic Components ‚Äî Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    15‚Üí- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    16‚Üí- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    17‚Üí- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    18‚Üí- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    19‚Üí- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    20‚Üí- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    21‚Üí- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    22‚Üí- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    23‚Üí- [Checklist](#checklist)
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí---
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí## Introduction
    28‚Üí
    29‚ÜíThe goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíKey decisions:
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    34‚Üí- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    35‚Üí- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    36‚Üí- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí---
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí## Components & Screens ‚Äî Hook Component Pattern
    41‚Üí
    42‚ÜíEach screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí```
    45‚Üíscreens/Home/
    46‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    47‚Üí‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    48‚Üí‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ Models/
    49‚Üí    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    50‚Üí```
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí**Models/index.d.ts**
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí```ts
    55‚Üíexport interface HomeParams {
    56‚Üí  welcome?: string;
    57‚Üí}
    58‚Üíexport interface HomeState {
    59‚Üí  greeting: string;
    60‚Üí  isLoading: boolean;
    61‚Üí}
    62‚Üíexport interface HomeHandlers {
    63‚Üí  onPressCTA: () => void;
    64‚Üí}
    65‚Üíexport type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    66‚Üí```
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí**index.ts (logic hook)**
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí```ts
    71‚Üíimport { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
    72‚Üíimport type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from './Models';
    73‚Üíimport { useUserStore } from '@/services/user/store'; // Zustand slice example
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üíexport function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    76‚Üí  const name = useUserStore(s => s.name);
    77‚Üí  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí  useEffect(() => {
    80‚Üí    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    81‚Üí    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    82‚Üí  }, []);
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí  const greeting = useMemo(
    85‚Üí    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || 'guest'}!`,
    86‚Üí    [
    87‚Üí      params?.welcome,
    88‚Üí      name,
    89‚Üí    ],
    90‚Üí  );
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    93‚Üí    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    94‚Üí  }, []);
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí  return {
    97‚Üí    greeting,
    98‚Üí    isLoading,
    99‚Üí    onPressCTA,
   100‚Üí  };

### Critical Findings for SDUI Implementation

**Web Compatibility Issues:**
1. **BackDropBottom** - Uses `react-native-modalize` (native gesture handlers)

   - SDUI adapters need web-safe alternatives (ScrollView/View)

2. **Loading** - Uses `lottie-react-native` (native Lottie bindings)
   - Mock exists but animations won't render in Storybook
   - SDUI adapters need fallback (ActivityIndicator/Text)

**Architecture Context:**
- This is an MFE consumed by SuperApp via yalc
- Store mock creates isolated Zustand instance for Storybook testing
- Path alias `~/store` redirected to mock prevents runtime dependencies
- Design System transpiled from TypeScript source (not built package)

**SDUI Context:**
According to RFC (.claude/rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt), voucher screens (SPMAIS-34653) are first SDUI implementation target. Components need to:
- Support JSON-driven rendering
- Handle variable interpolation (`{{var}}`)
- Support JSON Logic conditionals
- Map to existing DS components

### Summary

**Strengths:**
- Well-configured Storybook 8.6 with Vite
- Comprehensive DS validation story documenting web compatibility
- Proper mocks for native modules
- Theme provider wrapping all stories
- Automated screenshot generation with Playwright
- 235+ DS components available

**Gaps:**
- Only 2 stories currently (validation + smoke test)
- No SDUI component stories yet
- No screen-level stories
- BackDropBottom and Loading incompatible with web (documented)

**Next Steps for SDUI:**
- Create component registry mapping DS components to SDUI types
- Build adapters for BackDropBottom/Loading with web fallbacks
- Create Storybook stories for SDUI renderer with sample JSON payloads
- Test parameterizable screens in isolation
